name: Mixer Speaker
description: The `mixer` speaker platform allows you to mix audio sent to different source speakers into one output which is sent to another [speaker component](https://esphome.io/components/speaker/). Individual source speakers may be ducked (made quieter) with the [apply ducking action](https://esphome.io/components/speaker/mixer#mixer_speaker-apply_ducking).

config_entries.label: Output Speaker
config_entries.description: The [speaker](https://esphome.io/components/speaker/) to output the mixed audio.

config_entries.label: Source Speakers
config_entries.description: A list of source speaker inputs. Must have at least 2 and at most 8 speakers.

config_entries.label: Audio DAC
config_entries.description: The [audio DAC](https://esphome.io/components/audio_dac/) to use for volume control.

config_entries.label: Bits Per Sample
config_entries.description: The audio sample bit depth after resampling. Defaults to the output speaker's bits per sample.

config_entries.label: Buffer Duration
config_entries.description: The duration of the internal ring buffer. Larger values can reduce stuttering but use more memory. Defaults to `100ms`.

config_entries.label: Sample Rate
config_entries.description: Sample rate to convert to. Must be between `8000` and `48000`. Defaults to the output speaker's sample rate.

config_entries.label: Timeout
config_entries.description: How long to wait after finishing playback before releasing the bus. Set to `never` to never stop the speaker due to a timeout. Defaults to `500ms`.

config_entries.label: Num Channels
config_entries.description: The number of audio channels to send to the output speaker. Either `1` or `2`. Defaults to the output speaker's number of channels.

config_entries.label: Queue Mode
config_entries.description: Enables queue mode. If enabled, audio isn't mixed but instead each source speaker's audio is played successively, starting with the first listed source speaker.

config_entries.label: Task Stack In PSRAM
config_entries.description: Only with `esp-idf`. Run the audio tasks in external memory. Defaults to `false`.
